casual forest simu

Load packages

if (!require("pacman")){
  install.packages("pacman", repos = 'http://cran.us.r-project.org')
}
Loading required package: pacman
pacman::p_load("glmnet",
               "tidyverse",
               "rio",
               "magrittr",
               "broom",
               "labelled",
               "Hmisc",
               "flextable",
               "gtsummary",
               "emmeans",
               "here",
               "cleaR",
               "fastDummies",
               "formattable",
               "glue")
cleaR::clear()
{
  
    rmulti = function(prob, n) {
    as.numeric(rMultinom(probs=matrix(prob, 1, length(prob)), m=n))
  }
  
  expit <- function(linearcombination){
    n = (1/(1 + exp(-(linearcombination))))
    return (n)
  }
  
  logit <- function(pr){
    n = log(pr/(1-pr))
    return(n)
  }
  
round_df <- function(df, digits) {
  nums <- vapply(df, is.numeric, FUN.VALUE = logical(1))
  
  df[,nums] <- round(df[,nums], digits = digits)
  (df)
}

diff_fct <- function(x){
  dif <- x - lag(x)
  return(dif)
}


p_load("rlang")
recreate_var <- function(datau, var_name) {
  
  vars <- strsplit(var_name, "__")[[1]]
  
  expr <- rlang::parse_expr(paste(vars, collapse = " * "))
  
  datau %>% mutate("{var_name}" := !!expr)
}

geom_hist_percent <- function(.data, .var) {
  .data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = !!sym(.var))) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count/sum(count)))) +
    ylab("Percent") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    theme_bw()
}


}
n=10000
set.seed(123)

data_grf <- tibble(
  age   = rnorm(n, mean=68, sd=10), 
  black = rbinom(n, size=1, prob=0.3), 
  sbp   = rnorm(n, mean=140, sd=15), 
  dbp   = rnorm(n, mean=78, sd=12), 
  scr   = rnorm(n, mean=1.07, sd=0.34), 
  egfr  = rnorm(n, mean=72, sd=20), 
  egfr_ge72 = as.numeric(egfr>=72),
  statin=rbinom(n, size=1, prob=0.43), 
  aspirin=rbinom(n, size=1, prob=0.51), 
  fram  = rnorm(n, mean=25, sd=12), 
  smok  = factor(rmulti(prob=c(0.44, 0.42, 0.14), n))) %>% 
  dummy_cols("smok", remove_selected_columns = F,
             remove_first_dummy = T) %>% 
  mutate(
    tx = rbinom(n, size=1, prob=expit(logit(0.01) + 
                                        log(0.90)*statin+
                                        log(1.01)*sbp+
                                        log(1.01)*dbp+
                                        log(1.01)*age+
                                        log(1.03)*fram+
                                        log(1.1)*black+
                                        log(1.1)*scr+
                                        log(1.1)*smok_3 +
                                        log(1.05)*smok_2)),
    
    y = rbinom(n, size=1, prob=expit(logit(0.01) + 
                                       log(0.038)*tx + 
                                       log(0.592)*egfr_ge72 +
                                       log(0.453)*aspirin +
                                       log(12.00)*tx*egfr_ge72 + 
                                       log(34.70)*tx*aspirin +
                                       log(0.543)*egfr_ge72*aspirin + 
                                       log(0.372)*tx*egfr_ge72*aspirin +
                                       
                                       log(0.90)*statin+
                                       log(1.01)*sbp+
                                       log(1.01)*dbp+
                                       log(1.01)*age+
                                       log(1.03)*fram+
                                       log(1.1)*black+
                                       log(1.2)*scr+
                                       log(1.05)*smok_3 +
                                       log(1.05)*smok_2))) %>% 
  set_variable_labels(egfr    = "Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate",
                      aspirin = "Aspirin use",
                      statin  = "Statin use",
                      sbp     = "Systolic blood pressure",
                      dbp     = "Diastolic blood pressure",
                      age     = "Age in years",
                      fram    = "Framingham score risk",
                      black   = "Black race",
                      scr     = "Serum Creatinine",
                      smok_3  = "Current smoker", #smok_1: "Smoking (never)"
                      smok_2  = "Fomer smoker") 
library(haven)
library(labelled)



 

table1 <- data_grf %>% 
  mutate(tx = case_when(tx==1~"Treatment",
                        TRUE~"Placebo")) %>%
   mutate(`Smoking status` = as.numeric(as_factor(smok))) %>%
  dplyr::select(-contains("smok_"), -egfr_ge72, -smok) %>%
  relocate(y, .after = "Smoking status") %>%
  rename(`Y (cardiovascular events)` = y) %>%
  set_value_labels(`Smoking status` = c("Never smoker" = 1,
                            "Former smoker" = 2,
                            "Current smoker" =3)) %>%
  modify_if(is.labelled, to_factor) %>%
  tbl_summary(missing = "no",
              by="tx",
              digits = list(everything() ~ 0),
              statistic = list(all_continuous()  ~"{mean} ({sd})",
                               all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  add_stat_label(location = "row") %>%
  modify_header(label = "**Variable**") %>%
  modify_spanning_header(starts_with("stat_") ~ "Treatment Assignment") %>%
  bold_labels()


table1
Variable
Treatment Assignment

Diastolic blood pressure, Mean (SD) 78 (12) 78 (12) 79 (12)
Serum Creatinine, Mean (SD) 1 (0) 1 (0) 1 (0)
Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate, Mean (SD) 72 (20) 72 (20) 73 (20)
Statin use, n (%) 4,340 (43%) 3,094 (45%) 1,246 (41%)
Aspirin use, n (%) 5,117 (51%) 3,540 (51%) 1,577 (52%)
Framingham score risk, Mean (SD) 25 (12) 24 (12) 28 (12)
Smoking status, n (%)


    Never smoker 4,400 (44%) 3,074 (44%) 1,326 (43%)
    Former smoker 4,168 (42%) 2,905 (42%) 1,263 (41%)
    Current smoker 1,432 (14%) 965 (14%) 467 (15%)
Y (cardiovascular events), n (%) 1,808 (18%) 1,281 (18%) 527 (17%) 
library(tidyverse)
library(labelled)

final_data <- data_grf %>% 
  mutate(tx = case_when(tx == 1 ~ "Treatment",
                        TRUE ~ "Placebo")) %>%
  
  mutate(`Smoking status` = as_factor(smok)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-contains("smok_"), -egfr_ge72, -smok) %>%
  relocate(y, .after = `Smoking status`) %>%
  rename(`Y (cardiovascular events)` = y) %>%
  set_variable_labels(
    age     = "Age in years",
    black   = "Black race",
    sbp     = "Systolic blood pressure",
    dbp     = "Diastolic blood pressure",
    scr     = "Serum Creatinine",
    egfr    = "Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate",
    statin  = "Statin use",
    aspirin = "Aspirin use",
    fram    = "Framingham score risk",
    `Smoking status` = "Smoking status",
    `Y (cardiovascular events)` = "Y (cardiovascular events)",
    tx      = "Treatment Assignment"
  )%>% 
  mutate(across(c(black, statin, aspirin, `Y (cardiovascular events)`), 
                ~factor(.x, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No","Yes")))) %>%

  mutate(tx = factor(tx, levels = c("Placebo", "Treatment")))



glimpse(final_data)
Rows: 10,000
Columns: 12
$ age                         <dbl> 62.39524, 65.69823, 83.58708, 68.70508, 69…
$ black                       <fct> Yes, No, No, No, Yes, No, No, No, No, No, …
$ sbp                         <dbl> 119.6923, 131.3093, 127.0843, 154.5902, 14…
$ dbp                         <dbl> 68.45751, 64.37866, 84.96121, 84.21651, 80…
$ scr                         <dbl> 0.7680620, 1.1924899, 1.2135106, 0.8173015…
$ egfr                        <dbl> 91.44322, 67.53045, 74.94394, 67.48391, 83…
$ statin                      <fct> Yes, No, Yes, Yes, No, Yes, Yes, No, Yes, …
$ aspirin                     <fct> Yes, Yes, No, Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes, No,…
$ fram                        <dbl> 30.578637, 20.746942, 14.157178, 45.536461…
$ tx                          <fct> Treatment, Placebo, Placebo, Placebo, Plac…
$ `Smoking status`            <fct> 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 3, 2, 3, 1, 2, 2, 1, 3, 2, …
$ `Y (cardiovascular events)` <fct> Yes, No, No, No, Yes, No, No, No, No, No, …

Causal Forest part

options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org/"))
library(grf)
library(tidyverse)
# covert data to numeric
cf_data <- final_data %>% 
  mutate(
    tx = ifelse(tx == "Treatment", 1, 0),
    black = ifelse(black == "Yes", 1, 0),
    statin = ifelse(statin == "Yes", 1, 0),
    aspirin = ifelse(aspirin == "Yes", 1, 0),
    `Smoking status` = as.numeric(`Smoking status`), 
    y = ifelse(`Y (cardiovascular events)` == "Yes", 1, 0)
  ) %>% 
  select(-`Y (cardiovascular events)`)

casual forest model

set.seed(123)

Y <- cf_data$y
W <- cf_data$tx
X <- cf_data %>% select(-y, -tx)

cf_model <- causal_forest(X, Y, W)

predict cate

tau.hat <- predict(cf_model)$predictions
cf_data$tau.hat <- tau.hat
cf_data$tau.hat_pct <- tau.hat * 100

ITE distribution

geom_hist_percent <- function(.data, .var) {
  .data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = !!sym(.var))) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count/sum(count)))) +
    ylab("Percent") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    theme_bw()
}
cf_data %>%
  geom_hist_percent("tau.hat") +
  labs(x="Individualized treatement effects (ITE)") 
`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

plot Aspirin, eGFR and HTE

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

hte_cut <- 0

df_cf_plot <- cf_data %>%
  mutate(
    ite_pct = tau.hat * 100,
    aspirin_f = factor(aspirin, levels = c(0, 1),
                       labels = c("No Aspirin", "Aspirin"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(aspirin_f), !is.na(ite_pct))

ggplot(df_cf_plot, aes(x = aspirin_f, y = ite_pct)) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.15,
    height = 0,
    alpha = 0.55,
    size = 1.8,
    color = "#4C72B0"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = hte_cut,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "#D7191C"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Baseline Aspirin Use",
    y = "Estimated HTE (percentage points)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text  = element_text(color = "black")
  )
library(dplyr)
# count numbers in each observation
quad_counts <- df_cf_plot %>%
  mutate(
    quad = case_when(
      aspirin == 0 & ite_pct >= 0 ~ "Q1_left_up",
      aspirin == 0 & ite_pct <  0 ~ "Q3_left_low",
      aspirin == 1 & ite_pct >= 0 ~ "Q2_right_up",
      aspirin == 1 & ite_pct <  0 ~ "Q4_right_low"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(quad)
quad_counts
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  quad             n
  <chr>        <int>
1 Q2_right_up   5115
2 Q3_left_low   4883
3 Q4_right_low     2
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

cut_egfr <- 72.31
aspirin_bin <- 0.5
hte_cut   <- 0

df_cf2_plot <- cf_data %>%
  mutate(
    ite_pct = tau.hat * 100,
    aspirin_grp = factor(
      ifelse(aspirin >= 0.5, "Aspirin", "No Aspirin"),
      levels = c("No Aspirin", "Aspirin")
    ),
    quad = case_when(
      egfr <  cut_egfr & ite_pct >= hte_cut ~ "Q1_left_up",
      egfr >= cut_egfr & ite_pct >= hte_cut ~ "Q2_right_up",
      egfr <  cut_egfr & ite_pct <  hte_cut ~ "Q3_left_low",
      egfr >= cut_egfr & ite_pct <  hte_cut ~ "Q4_right_low"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(egfr), !is.na(ite_pct), !is.na(aspirin_grp))

quad_cols <- c(
  "Q1_left_up"  = "#8E87E8",
  "Q2_right_up" = "#F28E8C",
  "Q3_left_low" = "#8FD3B6",
  "Q4_right_low"= "#9AD0EC"
)


ggplot(df_cf2_plot,
       aes(x = egfr, y = ite_pct,
           color = quad,
           shape = factor(aspirin))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = cut_egfr, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = hte_cut, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = quad_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("0" = 16, "1" = 17),
    labels = c("No Aspirin", "Aspirin")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Baseline eGFR",
    y = "Estimated HTE (percentage points)",
    shape = "Aspirin use"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)
# count number
quad_N <- df_cf2_plot %>%
  count(quad)
quad_N
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  quad             n
  <chr>        <int>
1 Q1_left_up    2541
2 Q2_right_up   2574
3 Q3_left_low   2461
4 Q4_right_low  2424
cor_test_ite <- cor.test(
 cf_data$egfr,
  cf_data$tau.hat_pct,
  method = "spearman"
)

cor_test_ite

    Spearman's rank correlation rho

data:  cf_data$egfr and cf_data$tau.hat_pct
S = 1.0099e+11, p-value < 2.2e-16
alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
sample estimates:
      rho 
0.3940322

Variable importance within causal forest

vi <- variable_importance(cf_model)

vi_df <- tibble(
  variable = colnames(X),
  importance = vi
) %>%
  arrange(desc(importance)) %>%
  mutate(abs_coef = importance)   #


var_labels <- c(
  aspirin        = "Aspirin use",
  egfr           = "Estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR)",
  fram    = "Framingham risk score",
  statin         = "Statin use",
  scr     = "Serum creatinine",
  black          = "Black race",
  age            = "Age in years",
  dbp            = "Diastolic blood pressure",
  sbp = "Systolic blood pressure",
  former_smoker  = "Former smoker"
)


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(vi_df, aes(
  x = abs_coef,
  y = reorder(variable, abs_coef)
)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#377EB8") +   
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, max(vi_df$abs_coef), 0.1),
    limits = c(0, max(vi_df$abs_coef))
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = var_labels) +
  labs(
    x = "Variable importance",
    y = "Predictors",
    title = "Variable importance from Causal Forest"
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.2, linetype = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)

# Prepare dataset (exclude outcome, treatment, and redundant variables)
df_cart <- cf_data %>%
  select(-y, -tx, -tau.hat_pct, -tau.hat)

# Fit CART model using estimated treatment effects as outcome
cart_model <- rpart(
  tau.hat_pct ~ .,   # predict HTE (percentage scale)
  data = cbind(tau.hat_pct = cf_data$tau.hat_pct, df_cart),
  control = rpart.control(
    maxdepth = 2,    # limit tree depth for interpretability
    minbucket = 200  # minimum observations per terminal node
  )
)

# Plot tree to visualize subgroup cutoffs
rpart_cutoff <- rpart.plot(
  cart_model,
  type = 2,
  extra = 101,
  digits = 3,
  box.palette = "RdBu",
  shadow.col = "gray",
  cex = 0.8
)
# spearman correlation
cor_test <- cor.test(cf_data$egfr, cf_data$tau.hat_pct, method = "spearman")
cor_test

    Spearman's rank correlation rho

data:  cf_data$egfr and cf_data$tau.hat_pct
S = 1.0099e+11, p-value < 2.2e-16
alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
sample estimates:
      rho 
0.3940322 
# Estimate ATE from Causal Forest Predictions
# Compute ATE as the mean of individual treatment effects (tau.hat)
ate_rd_est <- mean(tau.hat)

# Convert ATE to percentage points
ate_rd_est_pct <- 100 * ate_rd_est


ate_rd_est
[1] -0.03084796
ate_rd_est_pct
[1] -3.084796
# Estimate ATE using GRF built-in function
ate_obj <- average_treatment_effect(cf_model, target.sample = "all")

# Handle different output formats (list vs numeric with attributes)
if (is.list(ate_obj)) {
  ate_se <- ate_obj$std.err        # standard error
  ate_est_check <- ate_obj$estimate
} else {
  ate_se <- attr(ate_obj, "std.err")
  ate_est_check <- as.numeric(ate_obj)
}

# Compare ATE from tau.hat vs GRF function
c(mean_tau = ate_rd_est, ate_from_grf = ate_est_check)
     mean_tau ate_from_grf1 ate_from_grf2 
 -0.030847965  -0.029281495   0.008122687 
ate_rd_ci <- ate_rd_est + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * ate_se


ate_rd_ci_pct <- 100 * ate_rd_ci

ate_rd_est_pct
[1] -3.084796
ate_rd_ci_pct
numeric(0)
# Fit separate regression forests for treated and control groups
rf1 <- regression_forest(X[W == 1, , drop = FALSE], Y[W == 1])
rf0 <- regression_forest(X[W == 0, , drop = FALSE], Y[W == 0])

# Predict potential outcomes for all individuals
p1_hat <- predict(rf1, X)$predictions   # predicted Y(1)
p0_hat <- predict(rf0, X)$predictions   # predicted Y(0)

# Compute marginal risks
P1 <- mean(p1_hat)   # E[Y(1)]
P0 <- mean(p0_hat)   # E[Y(0)]

# Compute risk ratio
rr_est <- P1 / P0
rr_est
[1] 0.8812392
# Confidence Interval for Risk Ratio (RR)
# Sample size
n <- length(Y)

# Variance of estimated marginal risks
var_P1 <- var(p1_hat) / n   # variance of E[Y(1)]
var_P0 <- var(p0_hat) / n   # variance of E[Y(0)]

# Delta method: standard error on log scale
se_log_rr <- sqrt(var_P1 / (P1^2) + var_P0 / (P0^2))

# Construct 95% CI for RR (log scale → exponentiate)
rr_ci <- exp(log(rr_est) + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * se_log_rr)

# Output RR estimate and confidence interval
rr_est
[1] 0.8812392
rr_ci
[1] 0.8678532 0.8948317

Interaction

df_int <- cf_data %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      aspirin == 0  & egfr < 71.3  ~ "G1",
      aspirin == 0  & egfr >= 71.3 ~ "G2",
      aspirin == 1 & egfr < 71.8 ~ "G3",
      aspirin == 1 & egfr >= 71.8 ~ "G4",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    group = factor(group, levels = c("G1","G2","G3","G4"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group))

table(df_int$group)  

  G1   G2   G3   G4 
2359 2524 2498 2619 
m0 <- glm(y ~ tx + group,
          data = df_int, family = binomial())

m1 <- glm(y ~ tx * group,
          data = df_int, family = binomial())

anova(m0, m1, test = "LRT")
Analysis of Deviance Table

Model 1: y ~ tx + group
Model 2: y ~ tx * group
  Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance  Pr(>Chi)    
1      9995     9373.4                          
2      9992     8760.0  3   613.42 < 2.2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
library(emmeans)


eff_within <- emmeans(m1, ~ tx | group) %>%
  contrast(method = "revpairwise")   # treat1 - treat0

eff_within
group = G1:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
 tx1 - tx0   -2.686 0.223 Inf -12.044  <.0001

group = G2:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
 tx1 - tx0   -0.704 0.125 Inf  -5.654  <.0001

group = G3:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
 tx1 - tx0    0.406 0.110 Inf   3.694  0.0002

group = G4:
 contrast  estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
 tx1 - tx0    1.988 0.129 Inf  15.460  <.0001

Results are given on the log odds ratio (not the response) scale. 
emmeans(m1, ~ tx * group) %>%
  contrast(
    interaction = "pairwise",
    by = NULL
  )
 tx_pairwise group_pairwise estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
 0 - 1       G1 - G2            1.98 0.255 Inf   7.757  <.0001
 0 - 1       G1 - G3            3.09 0.249 Inf  12.435  <.0001
 0 - 1       G1 - G4            4.67 0.257 Inf  18.156  <.0001
 0 - 1       G2 - G3            1.11 0.166 Inf   6.683  <.0001
 0 - 1       G2 - G4            2.69 0.179 Inf  15.038  <.0001
 0 - 1       G3 - G4            1.58 0.169 Inf   9.348  <.0001

Results are given on the log odds ratio (not the response) scale.

step 2 categorical subgroup

library(dplyr)

df_sub <- cf_data %>%
  mutate(
    ## Age: 2 groups: <60 / ≥60
    Age_cat = factor(if_else(age < 60, "<60", "≥60"),
                     levels = c("<60", "≥60")),

  
    ## Systolic BP (sbp_mean):  <120, 120–129.9, 130–139.9, 140–159.9, 160–179.9, ≥180
    SBP_cat = cut(
      sbp,
      breaks = c(-Inf, 120, 130, 140, 160, 180, Inf),
      labels = c("<120", "120–129.9", "130–139.9",
                 "140–159.9", "160–179.9", "≥180"),
      right = FALSE
    ),
    ## dbp:  <80, 80–89.9, ≥90
    DBP_cat = cut(
       dbp,
       breaks = c(-Inf, 80, 90, Inf),
       labels = c("<80", "80–89.9", "≥90"),
       right = FALSE
    ),
    
    # egfr: <15, 15–29.9, 30–44.9, 45–59.9, 60–89.9, ≥90
    egfr_cat = cut(
     egfr,
     breaks = c(-Inf, 15, 30, 45, 60, 90, Inf),
     labels = c("<15", "15–29.9", "30–44.9", "45–59.9", "60–89.9", "≥90"),
    right = FALSE
   ),
    ## framingham risk score: <10%, 10–19.9%, 20–29.9%, ≥30%
    fram_cat = cut(
      fram,
      breaks = c(-Inf, 10, 20, 30, Inf),
      labels = c("<10%", "10–19.9%", "20–29.9%", "≥30%"),
      right = FALSE
    ),
    
    scr_cat = factor(ifelse(scr >= 1.2, "≥1.2", "<1.2"))
  )

    
vars <- c("age","sbp","dbp","scr", "egfr","fram")
df_sub <- df_sub %>% select(-all_of(vars))
data.cart <- df_sub %>%
  select(
   -y, -tx, -tau.hat_pct, -tau.hat)
data.cart <- cbind(
  tau.hat_pct = df_sub$tau.hat_pct,
  data.cart
)
library(rpart)

cart_model <- rpart(
  tau.hat_pct ~ .,
  data = data.cart,
  control = rpart.control(maxdepth = 2, minbucket = 300)
)

library(rpart.plot)

rpart.plot(
  cart_model,
  type = 2,
  extra = 101,
  digits = 3,
  box.palette = "RdBu",
  shadow.col = "gray",
  cex = 0.75
)
g1 <- with(cf_data, aspirin == 0  & egfr < 59.9)             
g2 <- with(cf_data, aspirin == 0  & egfr >= 59.9)           
g3 <- with(cf_data, aspirin == 1 & egfr < 59.9)           
g4 <- with(cf_data, aspirin == 1 & egfr >= 59.9)         
library(grf)
library(dplyr)

subgroup_cf <- function(cf_model, subset_idx) {
  ate <- average_treatment_effect(
    cf_model,
    subset        = subset_idx,
    method        = "AIPW",
    target.sample = "all"
  )
  
  est <- ate[["estimate"]]
  se  <- ate[["std.err"]]
  
  tibble(
    tau      = est,
    se       = se,
    lwr      = est - 1.96 * se,
    upr      = est + 1.96 * se,
    p_value  = 2 * pnorm(-abs(est / se)),
    n        = sum(subset_idx)
  )
}

res_step3 <- bind_rows(
  G1 = subgroup_cf(cf_model, g1),
  G2 = subgroup_cf(cf_model, g2),
  G3 = subgroup_cf(cf_model, g3),
  G4 = subgroup_cf(cf_model, g4),
  .id = "group"
) %>%

  mutate(
    tau = tau * 100,
    se  = se  * 100,
    lwr = lwr * 100,
    upr = upr * 100
  )


res_step3 <- res_step3 %>%
  mutate(
    tau = round(tau, 2),
    se  = round(se, 2),
    lwr = round(lwr, 2),
    upr = round(upr, 2),
    p_value = round(p_value, 3)   
  )
flextable(res_step3)

summary table preparation

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(grf)

cutoff_egfr <- 60

# resubgroup based on catergorical cut off points
df_int <- cf_data %>%
  mutate(
    group4 = case_when(
      aspirin == 0 & egfr <  cutoff_egfr ~ paste0("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr),
      aspirin == 0 & egfr >= cutoff_egfr ~ paste0("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr),
      aspirin == 1 & egfr <  cutoff_egfr ~ paste0("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr),
      aspirin == 1 & egfr >= cutoff_egfr ~ paste0("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    group4 = factor(
      group4,
      levels = c(
        paste0("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr),
        paste0("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr),
        paste0("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr),
        paste0("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr)
      )
    ),
    idx = row_number()
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group4))

table(df_int$group4)

 G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60 G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60     G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60 
                     1318                      3565                      1377 
   G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60 
                     3740 
# check the subgroups
stopifnot(all(c("idx", "group4") %in% names(df_int)))
stopifnot(length(Y) >= max(df_int$idx))
stopifnot(length(W) >= max(df_int$idx))
stopifnot(nrow(X) >= max(df_int$idx))

n_total_train <- length(Y)

# subgroup labels
g1_label <- paste0("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr)
g2_label <- paste0("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr)
g3_label <- paste0("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<", cutoff_egfr)
g4_label <- paste0("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=", cutoff_egfr)


# RD: subgroup AIPW estimate from causal forest

subgroup_rd <- function(cf_model, subset_idx, n_total) {
  
  subset_idx <- subset_idx[!is.na(subset_idx)]
  
  if (length(subset_idx) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      RD = NA_real_,
      RD_se = NA_real_,
      RD_lwr = NA_real_,
      RD_upr = NA_real_,
      P_effect_RD = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  subset_full <- rep(FALSE, n_total)
  subset_full[subset_idx] <- TRUE
  
  ate <- tryCatch(
    average_treatment_effect(
      cf_model,
      subset = subset_full,
      method = "AIPW",
      target.sample = "all"
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  if (is.null(ate)) {
    return(tibble(
      RD = NA_real_,
      RD_se = NA_real_,
      RD_lwr = NA_real_,
      RD_upr = NA_real_,
      P_effect_RD = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  est <- ate[["estimate"]] * 100
  se  <- ate[["std.err"]] * 100
  
  tibble(
    RD = est,
    RD_se = se,
    RD_lwr = est - 1.96 * se,
    RD_upr = est + 1.96 * se,
    P_effect_RD = 2 * pnorm(-abs(est / se))
  )
}


#  RR: AIPW pseudo-outcome approach

rf1 <- regression_forest(X[W == 1, , drop = FALSE], Y[W == 1])
rf0 <- regression_forest(X[W == 0, , drop = FALSE], Y[W == 0])

m1_hat <- as.numeric(predict(rf1, X)$predictions)
m0_hat <- as.numeric(predict(rf0, X)$predictions)

eps  <- 1e-3
ehat <- pmin(pmax(cf_model$W.hat, eps), 1 - eps)

subgroup_rr <- function(subset_idx) {
  
  subset_idx <- subset_idx[!is.na(subset_idx)]
  
  if (length(subset_idx) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      RR = NA_real_, RR_lwr = NA_real_, RR_upr = NA_real_,
      logRR = NA_real_, se_logRR = NA_real_, P_effect_RR = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  Yi <- Y[subset_idx]
  Wi <- W[subset_idx]
  ei <- ehat[subset_idx]
  m1 <- m1_hat[subset_idx]
  m0 <- m0_hat[subset_idx]
  
  phi1 <- m1 + Wi * (Yi - m1) / ei
  phi0 <- m0 + (1 - Wi) * (Yi - m0) / (1 - ei)
  
  if (anyNA(phi1) || anyNA(phi0)) {
    return(tibble(
      RR = NA_real_, RR_lwr = NA_real_, RR_upr = NA_real_,
      logRR = NA_real_, se_logRR = NA_real_, P_effect_RR = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  mu1 <- mean(phi1)
  mu0 <- mean(phi0)
  
  if (!is.finite(mu1) || !is.finite(mu0) || mu0 <= 0 || mu1 <= 0) {
    return(tibble(
      RR = NA_real_, RR_lwr = NA_real_, RR_upr = NA_real_,
      logRR = NA_real_, se_logRR = NA_real_, P_effect_RR = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  
  IF1 <- phi1 - mu1
  IF0 <- phi0 - mu0
  
  cov10 <- cov(IF1, IF0) / length(subset_idx)
  se1 <- sd(IF1) / sqrt(length(subset_idx))
  se0 <- sd(IF0) / sqrt(length(subset_idx))
  
  var_logrr <- (se1^2) / (mu1^2) + (se0^2) / (mu0^2) - 2 * cov10 / (mu1 * mu0)
  se_logrr  <- sqrt(pmax(var_logrr, 0))
  
  rr <- mu1 / mu0
  logrr <- log(rr)
  
  tibble(
    RR = rr,
    RR_lwr = exp(logrr - 1.96 * se_logrr),
    RR_upr = exp(logrr + 1.96 * se_logrr),
    logRR = logrr,
    se_logRR = se_logrr,
    P_effect_RR = 2 * pnorm(-abs(logrr / se_logrr))
  )
}


#  P_diff functions

p_diff_rd <- function(estA, seA, estB, seB) {
  if (any(is.na(c(estA, seA, estB, seB)))) return(NA_real_)
  z <- (estA - estB) / sqrt(seA^2 + seB^2)
  2 * pnorm(-abs(z))
}

p_diff_logrr <- function(logA, seA, logB, seB) {
  if (any(is.na(c(logA, seA, logB, seB)))) return(NA_real_)
  z <- (logA - logB) / sqrt(seA^2 + seB^2)
  2 * pnorm(-abs(z))
}


subsets <- list(
  "N1: No aspirin parent" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 %in% c(g1_label, g2_label)) %>%
    pull(idx),
  
  "N2: Aspirin parent" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 %in% c(g3_label, g4_label)) %>%
    pull(idx),
  
  "G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 == g1_label) %>%
    pull(idx),
  
  "G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 == g2_label) %>%
    pull(idx),
  
  "G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 == g3_label) %>%
    pull(idx),
  
  "G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60" = df_int %>%
    filter(group4 == g4_label) %>%
    pull(idx)
)

sapply(subsets, length)
    N1: No aspirin parent        N2: Aspirin parent  G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60 
                     4883                      5117                      1318 
G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60     G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60    G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60 
                     3565                      1377                      3740 
#  RD / RR

tab4_raw <- lapply(names(subsets), function(nm) {
  
  idx <- subsets[[nm]]
  
  rd <- subgroup_rd(cf_model, idx, n_total_train)
  rr <- subgroup_rr(idx)
  
  tibble(
    subgroup = nm,
    N = length(idx),
    Proportion = 100 * length(idx) / nrow(df_int),
    
    RD = rd$RD,
    RD_se = rd$RD_se,
    RD_lwr = rd$RD_lwr,
    RD_upr = rd$RD_upr,
    P_effect_RD = rd$P_effect_RD,
    
    RR = rr$RR,
    RR_lwr = rr$RR_lwr,
    RR_upr = rr$RR_upr,
    logRR = rr$logRR,
    se_logRR = rr$se_logRR,
    P_effect_RR = rr$P_effect_RR
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

tab4_raw
# A tibble: 6 × 14
  subgroup     N Proportion     RD RD_se RD_lwr RD_upr P_effect_RD     RR RR_lwr
  <chr>    <int>      <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
1 N1: No …  4883       48.8 -20.0   1.01 -22.0   -18.1    8.05e-87 0.265  0.221 
2 N2: Asp…  5117       51.2  13.4   1.21  11.0    15.8    2.52e-28 2.24   1.97  
3 G1: No …  1318       13.2 -29.0   1.70 -32.4   -25.7    1.25e-65 0.0655 0.0329
4 G2: No …  3565       35.6 -16.7   1.24 -19.1   -14.3    1.05e-41 0.355  0.293 
5 G3: Asp…  1377       13.8   7.31  2.36   2.68   11.9    1.96e- 3 1.50   1.19  
6 G4: Asp…  3740       37.4  15.6   1.41  12.9    18.4    1.87e-28 2.67   2.29  
# ℹ 4 more variables: RR_upr <dbl>, logRR <dbl>, se_logRR <dbl>,
#   P_effect_RR <dbl>
# P_diff

get <- function(x) tab4_raw %>% filter(subgroup == x)

Pdiff_top_RD <- p_diff_rd(
  get("N1: No aspirin parent")$RD,
  get("N1: No aspirin parent")$RD_se,
  get("N2: Aspirin parent")$RD,
  get("N2: Aspirin parent")$RD_se
)

Pdiff_left_RD <- p_diff_rd(
  get("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60")$RD,
  get("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60")$RD_se,
  get("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60")$RD,
  get("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60")$RD_se
)

Pdiff_right_RD <- p_diff_rd(
  get("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60")$RD,
  get("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60")$RD_se,
  get("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60")$RD,
  get("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60")$RD_se
)

Pdiff_top_RR <- p_diff_logrr(
  get("N1: No aspirin parent")$logRR,
  get("N1: No aspirin parent")$se_logRR,
  get("N2: Aspirin parent")$logRR,
  get("N2: Aspirin parent")$se_logRR
)

Pdiff_left_RR <- p_diff_logrr(
  get("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60")$logRR,
  get("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60")$se_logRR,
  get("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60")$logRR,
  get("G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60")$se_logRR
)

Pdiff_right_RR <- p_diff_logrr(
  get("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60")$logRR,
  get("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60")$se_logRR,
  get("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60")$logRR,
  get("G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60")$se_logRR
)


# summary  tab4

tab4 <- tab4_raw %>%
  mutate(
    P_diff_RD = case_when(
      grepl("^N1|^N2", subgroup) ~ Pdiff_top_RD,
      subgroup %in% c("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60", "G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60") ~ Pdiff_left_RD,
      subgroup %in% c("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60", "G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60") ~ Pdiff_right_RD
    ),
    P_diff_RR = case_when(
      grepl("^N1|^N2", subgroup) ~ Pdiff_top_RR,
      subgroup %in% c("G1: No aspirin & eGFR<60", "G2: No aspirin & eGFR>=60") ~ Pdiff_left_RR,
      subgroup %in% c("G3: Aspirin & eGFR<60", "G4: Aspirin & eGFR>=60") ~ Pdiff_right_RR
    ),
    
    RD = ifelse(
      is.na(RD),
      "NA (NA, NA)",
      sprintf("%.2f (%.2f, %.2f)", RD, RD_lwr, RD_upr)
    ),
    RR = ifelse(
      is.na(RR),
      "NA (NA, NA)",
      sprintf("%.2f (%.2f, %.2f)", RR, RR_lwr, RR_upr)
    ),
    Proportion = sprintf("%.2f", Proportion),
    
    P_effect_RD = ifelse(
      is.na(P_effect_RD),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(P_effect_RD < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", P_effect_RD))
    ),
    P_effect_RR = ifelse(
      is.na(P_effect_RR),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(P_effect_RR < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", P_effect_RR))
    ),
    P_diff_RD = ifelse(
      is.na(P_diff_RD),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(P_diff_RD < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", P_diff_RD))
    ),
    P_diff_RR = ifelse(
      is.na(P_diff_RR),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(P_diff_RR < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", P_diff_RR))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    subgroup, N, Proportion,
    RD, P_effect_RD, P_diff_RD,
    RR, P_effect_RR, P_diff_RR
  )
